results.rendered_html: citra—kūṭa m. ‘wonderful peak’, N. of a hill and district (the modern Citrakote or Catarkot near Kāmtā, situated on the river Paisuni about 50 miles S.E. of the Bandah in Bundelkhund; first habitation of the exiled Rāma and Lakṣmaṇa, crowded with temples as the holiest spot of Rāma's worshippers),  MBh. iii, 8200 ;  R. i -iii ;  Ragh. xii f. ;  VarBṛS. ;  BhP.
